metadata.purpose: Assess prerequisite knowledge before learning about place value up to 5 digits, reading and writing large numbers, comparing and ordering, and estimation

prerequisiteConcepts.conceptName: Place value of 3-digit numbers (ones, tens, hundreds)
Counting forward and backward up to 1000
General number sense and understanding of number magnitude

questions.question: In the number 347, what is the value of the digit 3?
questions.explanation: The digit 3 is in the hundreds place. So its value is 3 hundreds = 300.

commonMistakes.mistake: Gave the face value instead of the place value
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding the difference between face value and place value
commonMistakes.remediation: Practice identifying place value by saying 'the 3 is in the hundreds place, so it means 300'

questions.question: Which digit is in the tens place in the number 582?
questions.explanation: In 582, the digit 2 is in the ones place, 8 is in the tens place, and 5 is in the hundreds place.

commonMistakes.mistake: Confused the hundreds place with the tens place
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Knowing the position names from right to left
commonMistakes.remediation: Start from the right: ones, tens, hundreds. Practice with a place value chart.

questions.question: What is the expanded form of 269?
questions.explanation: In 269, the 2 is worth 200, the 6 is worth 60, and the 9 is worth 9. So expanded form is 200 + 60 + 9.

commonMistakes.mistake: Wrote face values instead of place values
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding that expanded form uses the value of each digit based on its position
commonMistakes.remediation: Use a place value chart: write each digit under hundreds, tens, and ones columns

questions.question: What number comes just after 499?
questions.explanation: The number just after 499 is 500. When we add 1 to 499, we get 500.

commonMistakes.mistake: Changed the wrong digit when adding 1
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding how carrying works when ones and tens are both 9

questions.question: Count by 100s: 400, 500, 600, __. What comes next?
questions.explanation: When we count by 100s, we add 100 each time. 600 + 100 = 700.

questions.question: What number is 10 more than 990?
questions.explanation: 990 + 10 = 1000. This is how we move from 3-digit numbers to 4-digit numbers!

commonMistakes.remediation: Practice adding 10 to numbers near 1000 using a number line

questions.question: Which number is greater: 456 or 465?
questions.explanation: Both have 4 in the hundreds place. Looking at the tens place: 465 has 6 tens while 456 has 5 tens. 6 > 5, so 465 is greater.

commonMistakes.mistake: Compared ones digits instead of tens digits
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Not comparing from the highest place value first
commonMistakes.remediation: Always start comparing from the leftmost (highest) place value

questions.question: Arrange these numbers in ascending order (smallest to largest): 734, 743, 347
questions.explanation: 347 is the smallest (3 hundreds). Then 734 (7 hundreds, 3 tens). Then 743 (7 hundreds, 4 tens). So ascending order is 347, 734, 743.

commonMistakes.mistake: Arranged in descending order instead of ascending
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Confusing ascending (smallest to largest) with descending (largest to smallest)
commonMistakes.remediation: Remember: ascending means going UP like climbing stairs

questions.question: What is the largest 3-digit number you can make using the digits 5, 3, and 8 (each used once)?
questions.explanation: To make the largest number, put the biggest digit in the hundreds place (8), then the next biggest in tens (5), then the smallest in ones (3). The answer is 853.

commonMistakes.mistake: Put digits in descending order but mixed up tens and ones
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Not systematically placing digits from largest to smallest
commonMistakes.remediation: First sort the digits from biggest to smallest, then write them left to right

questions.question: Which of these numbers is closest to 500?
questions.explanation: Find how far each number is from 500: 389 is 111 away, 478 is 22 away, 512 is 12 away, 550 is 50 away. 512 is only 12 away from 500, so it is the closest.

commonMistakes.mistake: Chose a number less than 500 thinking it must be closer
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding that numbers both above and below can be close to a target
commonMistakes.remediation: Calculate the difference from 500 for each option and compare

excellent.message: Fantastic! You have a strong understanding of 3-digit place value and counting. You are ready to explore thousands and even bigger numbers!

good.message: Great job! You know your 3-digit numbers well. Let's learn about thousands and 5-digit numbers!

partial.message: You know some basics about place value, but let's review a few things first. Watch the review videos and you will be all set for big numbers!

needs_review.message: No worries! Let's first watch some fun videos about 3-digit numbers and counting to 1000. Then you will be ready to learn about thousands and even bigger numbers!

feedbackMessages.on_completion: Great job completing the readiness check!
feedbackMessages.low_score_encouragement: Don't worry! We have some fun videos to help you get ready. Big numbers are exciting and you are going to love learning about thousands!
feedbackMessages.high_score_praise: Awesome! You are a place value pro! Let's discover numbers in the thousands and beyond!
feedbackMessages.retake_option: After watching the recommended videos, you can try again to check your progress.
